clues.text: 1.The galleon, the ship built from oak, and the ship built from walnut are three different models.
2.The 18 inch ship is built from maple.
3.William's ship, the brigantine, and the ship built from walnut are three different models.
4.The ship built from walnut, the brigantine, and Arthur's ship are three different models.
7.Of the 30 inch model and Charles's model, one is built from maple and the other is a galleon.

puzzles.story: Russell spent a rainy afternoon at the Westdale Marine Museum, marveling at the intricate model ships displayed in glass cases, each one a miniature masterpiece of rigging and hull work.  Match each model to its type and the kind of wood it was made of, as well as the person who made it, and determine the size of each model. Remember, as with all grid-based logic puzzles, no option in any category will ever be used more than once.  If you get stuck or run into problems, try the "Clear Errors" button to remove any mistakes that might be present on the grid, or the "Hint" button to see the next logical step in the puzzle.
